← the Atlas

Remote terminals over SSH

Features·budding·accepted·JUN 26, 2026

The skeleton plan for kolu#951 — remote terminals over SSH, as one R1–R10 phase tree. R1–R4 shipped (foundation · local kaval survivor · the remote-ssh spike · the standalone pulam awareness daemon), R5 shipped (the overflow-recovery loop — a typed overflow frame so a dropped slow subscriber re-attaches instead of freezing), R6 shipped (the @kolu/terminal-workspace library — one fs/git impl, two homes) and R7 shipped (mirrorRemoteSurface is now a total dual, procedures forwarded); the remaining work R8 (kolu serves the shared awareness) · R9 (dial remotes) · R10 (canvas) is a linear roadmap defined in this note. Build logs live in the child notes — pty-daemon (R2), kaval-sessions (R3), pulam (R4).

Contents

  • User-facing description
  • Architecture-level changes
  • Implementation details
    • R5 — overflow-recovery loop
    • R-dock-unify — kolu’s Dock reads the shared agentProjection
    • R-pip-unify — one status pip across the web surfaces
    • R-activity-merge — merge the activity dot into the state pip
    • R6 — @kolu/terminal-workspace: one library, one fs/git impl, two homes
    • R7 — mirrorRemoteSurface is now a total dual — shipped
    • R8 — kolu serves awareness from terminalWorkspaceSurface
    • R9 — kolu dials remotes
    • R10 — canvas multiplex + validate
  • History

The skeleton plan for #951 — remote terminals over SSH. Every host — this machine and each ssh remote — runs the same kaval daemon; kolu-server keys its terminal endpoint by hostId, and you switch hosts from the ChromeBar, kinda like tmux sessions. R1–R7 are shipped — the foundation, the local kaval survivor, the remote-ssh spike, the standalone pulam awareness daemon, the overflow-recovery loop (R5 — a dropped slow subscriber re-attaches instead of freezing), @kolu/terminal-workspace (one fs/git impl, two homes; the surface is pulam’s until R8), and the total-dual surface mirror (procedures forwarded). What remains (R8 · R9 · R10) is one linear roadmap, defined in this note: serve one shared surface, dial remotes and wire those into the tile, then multiplex the canvas. The shipped branches’ build logs live in the child notes: pty-daemon (R2), kaval-sessions (R3), pulam (R4).

User-facing description

kolu
hostslocalnix@prodstaging+ host⟳ live
local
kolu· master
◜codexworking
nix@prodssh
kolu· feat/dial-ssh#1412 ✓
●claudeawaiting you
nix@prodssh
infra· deploy
◜—working
local
notes· main
○claudeidle

You switch hosts from the ChromeBar — kinda like tmux sessions — and a remote host’s terminals sit on the canvas beside your local ones. A remote tile wears an ssh badge and carries the same live awareness a local tile does — git branch/dirty, PR status, agent state, and a green dot when it’s moving bytes — and it survives a network blip or a kolu-server deploy with full scrollback. One local host is always present; remotes are added on demand, auto-detected from your ssh config.

Architecture-level changes

One endpoint, bound to a dialed kaval. A kaval is a daemon you dial — local over a unix socket, remote over ssh — so remote is not a second backend. There is one TerminalEndpoint, bound to a HostLocation-resolved kaval endpoint ({kind:"local"} today, {kind:"remote",host} in R9). That resolver is the sole place a tile maps to its kaval; everything downstream talks to the backend and never asks “which kind?”. No RemoteTerminalEndpoint — R9 adds the ssh driver as an additive sibling of R2’s local driver, never a retrofit.

Two volatility axes stay open (R1/R2 closed the rest):

Axis — changes for its own reason Encapsulated by
Where a terminal’s state lives — this machine vs an ssh host the HostLocation-resolved kaval endpoint — one impl + a discriminator, not a second backend
How the backend reaches its agent — transport · framing · reconnect HostSession + @kolu/surface/links/stdio — unix socket today, ssh in R9

Zero domain knowledge crosses the transport (Hickey’s cut): the agent runs the unmodified sensors and the boundary ships their values. An earlier per-domain design (RemoteGitInfoProvider, …) was transport adapters wearing domain costumes; the single-endpoint seam dissolved them.

kolu keeps its own copy of awareness — R8 deletes it

Today the sensors write awareness into kolu’s own terminalMetadata record — a private copy. R8 has them write the shared terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness instead, and kolu (and the browser) just read it through one seam (awarenessFor). One copy, one writer. Detail in R8.

Already paid for, so R5–R10 are narrow. @kolu/surface-nix-host ships HostSession, provisionAgent (ships the derivation — a darwin parent drives a linux remote with no cross-builder), and resolveSystem; @kolu/surface ships mirrorRemoteSurface (now a total dual). R2 already shipped the host-count-agnostic spine: @kolu/surface-daemon-supervisor and the per-connect system.version handshake. So R9 is an ssh driver behind shipped seams; R8 makes kolu serve the shared awareness; R7 completed the surface mirror (#1505); R6 grew pulam’s fs/git (#1506); R5 added the one wire signal that distinguishes a slow-subscriber drop from a PTY exit (#1591).

Implementation details

The whole epic as one tree — bird’s-eye, with each row’s right-hand link a drill-down to where that phase lives (the child notes for shipped branches, the ### sections below for the rest). R1–R7 (incl. pulam-web’s framework) are shipped; R8–R10 remain.

ROADMAP — remote terminals over ssh (#951)✓ shipped · ▶ do next · ◐ build-clean · ○ todo · → drill in
✓R1 · Foundation — seam · framework · engine#981·984·1004
✓R2 · kaval — local PTY survival→ pty-daemon
✓R2.1–R2.5 · inversion → door → survival → inventory#1292…1458
✓R3 · remote-ssh spike→ kaval-sessions
✓R3.1–R3.6 · kaval-tui dials local + ssh#1364…1378
✓R4 · pulam — the workspace daemon→ pulam
✓R4.1–R4.5 · sensors → daemon → --host → fleet#1413…1497
✓R4.6 · rename arivu → pulam#1512
✓R4.7 · live git status in pulam-tui#1519
◐R4.8 · pulam-web — the browser twin→ pulam-web
✓R-pulamweb-1 · drishti reactive consumerdrishti#72
✓R-pulamweb-2 · framework — provision · fan-out · list#1524
✓R-pulamweb-3 · agent dashboard — agents by state#1535
○R-pulamweb-4 · git status — dirty cell + drill-in (live file tree)after R-pulamweb-3 · proves the remote leg
✓R5 · Overflow-recovery loop — typed overflow frame, web tier re-attaches#1591
✓R-dock-unify · kolu's Dock reads the shared agentProjection (consistency)#1541
✓R-pip-unify · one status pip — Dock + dashboard render the same icon#1551
✓R-activity-merge · merge the activity dot into the state pip, one shared indicator#1555
✓R6 · terminal-workspace — one fs/git impl, two homes#1506
✓R7 · mirrorRemoteSurface → total dual#1505
▶R8 · kolu serves terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness (drop its copy)◀ DO NEXT
○R9 · kolu dials remotesblocked-on R8 ↓
○R9a · localhost dedup — one sensor set (fixes the live state desync)needs R8 served ↓
○R10 · canvas multiplex + validatedefined ↓

Everything marked ✓ is shipped (PRs linked). pulam-web’s agent dashboard (R-pulamweb-3) just shipped (#1535) — every agent across the fleet, by state, a render layer on already-shipped data. The live edge is now R8 — kolu still keeps its own copy of awareness (the sensors write a private terminalMetadata record), duplicating the awareness collection the daemon already serves on terminalWorkspaceSurface. R8 deletes the copy so kolu reads the shared one — a clean, self-contained compose that unblocks R9a. (kolu’s koluSurface fs/git streams are a separate, bigger move — rewiring the Code tab from value-bearing streams to the shared surface’s procedure + pulse shape, see surface live data — so that rides R9, not R8.) In parallel: R5 (overflow) shipped independently (#1591); R-pulamweb-4 (the live git drill-in) consumes the shared surface’s git procedure + pulse and proves the remote leg for R9; R9 is blocked-on R8 + R-pulamweb-4; R10 needs R9. The web-UI thread that single-sources the per-row indicators has now landed in full: R-dock-unify (#1541) made the semantics one source (kolu’s Dock is the third consumer of the shared agentProjection), R-pip-unify (#1551) made the state pip one component, and R-activity-merge (#1555) merged the last two drifted axes — the live-activity dot and the unread alert — into that same StatePip as a sweeping green ring + an amber corner badge, so each row now renders one indicator from one leaf. The live state-desync users actually see is now R9a — localhost dedup, one sensor set (these semantic/visual unifies sit downstream of it and can’t fix a two-derivation problem). Each row drills into its detail — the child notes for shipped branches, the ### sections below for the rest.

R5 — overflow-recovery loop

R5· overflow-recovery loop✓ shipped
links #1591

Independent — needed nothing, blocked nothing; landed on its own.

Shipped (#1591). kaval sheds a slow attach subscriber by ending its iterator, which was indistinguishable on the wire from a PTY exit — so the client treated the drop as terminal and froze scrollback. The attach contract (ptyHostSurface, bumped to 4.0 — a new emitted union variant is breaking for an older client, which the version predicate would otherwise wave through) now carries a typed overflow control frame, emitted as the stream’s last frame when the host drops a lagging subscriber, distinct from a PTY exit. The web tier (terminalEndpoint/local.ts) reads it and re-attaches for a fresh snapshot — reset-then-snapshot so the repaint replaces stale rows rather than double-painting — instead of freezing; kaval-tui’s existing re-attach loop reads it to skip writing a dataless frame; pulam ignores it (activity is best-effort). The drop is reproducible on the local socket, so it is CI-tested today (inProcessPtyHost.test.ts, reattachingDeltas.test.ts), and the hardened contract is a clean prerequisite for the remote reconnect R8/R9 lean on. herdr vs kolu flagged the same gap.

R-dock-unify — kolu’s Dock reads the shared agentProjection

R-dock-unify· Dock + dashboard, one source✓ shipped
needs ← R-pulamweb-3links born in #1535 · #1541

Shipped (#1541) — kolu’s Dock became the third consumer of the shared @kolu/terminal-workspace/agentProjection, so the Dock, pulam-tui, and pulam-web rank/paint agents from one source; a differential test pins them equal.

R-pip-unify — one status pip across the web surfaces

R-pip-unify· Dock + dashboard render the same icon✓ shipped
needs ← R-dock-unifylinks #1551

Shipped (#1551) — the Dock’s StatePip lifted into @kolu/solid-statepip + a shared @kolu/theme, so the Dock and pulam-web render the identical status pip from one component.

R-activity-merge — merge the activity dot into the state pip

R-activity-merge· merge the activity dot into the state pip✓ shipped
needs ← R-pip-unifylinks #1555

Shipped (#1555) — the live-activity dot and the unread alert merged into that same StatePip (a sweeping green ring + an amber corner badge), so each row renders one indicator from one leaf.

R6 — @kolu/terminal-workspace: one library, one fs/git impl, two homes

R6· terminal-workspace — one fs/git impl, two homes✓ shipped
needs ← R4blocks → R8links #1506

Shipped (#1506) — @kolu/terminal-workspace: one fs/git impl (createTerminalWorkspaceEndpoint), run in-process by kolu and hosted remotely by pulam. The shared impl, not yet the shared surface — the shared awareness surface is R8; the fs/git surface is R9.

R7 — mirrorRemoteSurface is now a total dual — shipped #1505

R7· mirrorRemoteSurface → total dual✓ shipped
blocks → R9links #1505

Shipped (#1505) — mirrorRemoteSurface became a total dual returning { procedures, done } (every procedure a forwarding stub), so serve ∘ mirror ≈ identity; proven by drishti’s forwarded “Kill process” action under the surface.md gate.

R8 — kolu serves awareness from terminalWorkspaceSurface

R8· kolu serves terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness; drops its awareness copy○ todo
needs ← R6blocks → R9 · R9a

Today kolu keeps its own copy of awareness — the sensors write it into kolu’s private terminalMetadata record — duplicating the awareness collection the pulam daemon already serves on terminalWorkspaceSurface. R8 deletes the copy: the sensors write terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness, and kolu reads it through one seam (awarenessFor). kolu keeps only what it authors (id · restore-cwd · theme · layout · sleeping-state). One writer per fact.

It’s clean and self-contained — terminalWorkspaceSurface already declares the awareness collection (no shape change), so the work is small: serve it from kolu, point the single sensor-writer at it, add awarenessFor, slim terminalMetadata. Done in-process, directly in kolu. It unblocks R9a (the localhost state-desync) by giving kolu a served awareness a second process can read, and makes remote awareness in R9 a backing-swap behind the same awarenessFor.

fs/git is not part of R8 — it rides R9. terminalWorkspaceSurface deliberately serves fs/git as procedures + pulse, not value-bearing streams: fs.listAll/git.getStatus are request→response procedures, and subscribeRepoChange/subscribeFileChange are payload-free {seq} pulses you re-query on (surface.ts:89-109 — “re-queries procedures rather than streaming full diffs over the wire”). kolu’s Code tab today reads koluSurface’s value-bearing streams (app.streams.gitStatus/fsListAll/gitDiff.use, CodeTab.tsx:314-373). Making kolu read the shared surface’s fs/git means rewriting the Code tab from value-bearing streams to procedure + pulse-then-requery — a large client change the surface ties to mirroring the surface whole. So it lands with R9, where kolu mirrors the whole surface anyway.

R9 — kolu dials remotes

R9· kolu dials remotes○ todo
needs ← R7 · R8 · R-pulamweb-4blocks → R10

The linchpin — it needs R7 (the total mirror) and R8 (kolu already serves the one terminalWorkspaceSurface). kolu-server gains the N-endpoint registry + the ssh driver — the {kind:"remote",host} arm, the (host,id) join, a persisted location — reusing the same getHostSession the spike (R3) built. It dials the host’s kaval for the PTY (via the shipped frontDaemonOverStdio) → a remote tile; and dials its pulam over a long-lived HostSession, mirroring the whole terminal-workspace surface (R7) into one local handle. Because R8 already moved awareness onto that surface, R9 just points the remote terminal’s dispatch at the mirror instead of the in-process impl. R9 is also where the Code tab’s fs/git moves off koluSurface’s value-bearing streams onto the shared surface’s procedures + {seq} pulse (re-query on each pulse — the surface’s deliberate remote shape, see surface live data), deleting the last koluSurface reads. The mirror’s streams — awareness activity + the fs/git {seq} watchers — re-serve to the browser over the same websocketLink + surfaceClient leg R-pulamweb-4 proves (and R9 turns the quiet activity stream live on it), so R-pulamweb-4’s pulam-web graduation gates R9. location is persisted kolu-side, re-validated on reconnect against the remote kaval’s live cwd. (Paste/upload is a TerminalEndpoint concern — the same seam, the PRECONDITION_FAILED guard.) The result: a remote terminal as capable as a local one — PTY, awareness badge, green dot, working Code tab. The shape is the lead diagram above.

Adoption — a dial joins, never clobbers. If the host already runs a kaval it is adopted (the same adoptOrEnsure spine the local boot uses); a duplicate is impossible by construction (the pid-gate’s atomic link(2) EEXIST). On dial:

the remote kaval is… kolu does
absent provision + spawn fresh (adoptOrEnsure → ensure)
live · wire-compatible adopt — connect, never kill; its PTYs reconcile in
live · a build behind adopt + an “update pending” nudge (orthogonal)
live · contract-skewed recycle (kill → respawn) — only on a typed DaemonContractSkewError
unreachable (bad dial) bounded retry, then degraded — never killed

One kaval per remote host: dialing joins its terminals (“the prod terminals”), never racing to kill them.

R9a — localhost dedup (the desync the dashboard shows). Under PULAM_WEB_HOSTS=localhost, pulam-web spawns its own pulam, so two independent sensor sets observe the same terminal and never reconcile — the working-in-pulam-web vs idle-in-Dock split. The fix is R9’s backing-swap applied locally: don’t spawn a second pulam — point pulam-web’s awarenessFor at kolu’s served awareness. One sensor, two readers. Prereq: R8 must serve terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness cross-process (not only read it in-process), or there’s nothing for the second process to read.

R10 — canvas multiplex + validate

R10· canvas multiplex + validate○ todo
needs ← R9

The UI: an active-kaval strip (local always present), a per-tile host hint, an ssh-config host picker — kinda like tmux sessions. Then validate the feel on real use: does N-kavals-at-once beat tmux-style one-at-a-time switching? Fall back to switch if multiplex doesn’t earn its keep. The granularity call lands here, validated, not up front.

History

  • R5 shipped — overflow-recovery loop (2026-06-26, #1591) — the attach contract (ptyHostSurface 4.0 — a breaking major bump, since a new emitted union variant is not backwards-compatible for an older client) gained a typed overflow control frame; the web tier re-attaches for a fresh snapshot on it instead of freezing scrollback, distinct from a PTY exit. Hardens the attach contract for both transports ahead of R8/R9.
  • R8 re-scoped to awareness only; fs/git is R9 (2026-06-26) — a fresh implementing agent caught that terminalWorkspaceSurface deliberately serves fs/git as procedures + a {seq} pulse (not value-bearing streams; surface.ts:89-109). So R8 is now just the awareness compose (clean, unblocks R9a); making kolu read the shared surface’s fs/git is the Code-tab rewrite to pulse-then-requery, which rides R9. R-pulamweb-4 consumes the existing procedure + pulse (needs only R-pulamweb-3). The two wire shapes are explained in surface live data.
  • Pierre de-gated (2026-06-26) — the @pierre/trees swallow-emit was reproduced harmless in kolu (#1534), so the git drill-in no longer waits on a renderer proof; R-pulamweb-4 consumes the existing procedure + pulse.
  • R-activity-merge (2026-06-24, #1555) — activity dot + unread alert merged into StatePip.
  • R-pip-unify (2026-06-23, #1551) — one status pip via @kolu/solid-statepip + @kolu/theme.
  • R-dock-unify (2026-06-23, #1541) — Dock joins the shared agentProjection.
  • codex late-detection fixed (2026-06-24, #1559) — the kaval pty-host retains lastCommand and replays commandRun snapshot-first, so a late/restarted sensor detects command-only agents.
  • R-pulamweb-1/2/3 (2026-06-22 → 23) — pulam-web shipped: the reactive stream consumer (drishti #72), the framework (#1524), and the agent dashboard (#1535).
  • R7 (2026-06-21, #1505) — mirrorRemoteSurface total dual.
  • R6 (2026-06-21, #1506) — @kolu/terminal-workspace: one fs/git impl, two homes.
  • R1–R4 (2026-05-26 → 06-21) — the TerminalEndpoint seam, @kolu/surface-nix-host, the shared sensor engine, the pty-daemon multi-client survivor, and the arivu→pulam rename.
